lines: Tobias watches the road through a broken shutter.
"No boot marks since before the knives. Not Remnant boots, not wall boots. Something swallowed the road before the cult found us."

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: Tobias can repair the chapel bell if you bring him to the frame.

choices.label: Ask why he listened to Susanna
Ask what he would do next

lines: Tobias has the look of a man who counted exits until the numbers stopped helping.
"I ran messages to the Hallowfen outposts on the same route every week. Then the bells quit answering, and Susanna put one finger in my face until I agreed not to go alone."
"For once I listened."
"The route is simple if nothing wants you. Chapel gate, dry ditch, broken toll post, then the wall road."
"Simple routes kill more people than complicated ones. A man stops watching because the ground has behaved itself before."
"Give me room at the frame. The old pin split clean, which means some tidy monster wanted this bell quiet."
"Let me set it before you pull that rope. If it tears loose, it takes fingers with it."
"I kept the gate pulleys alive when the wardens still paid in soup and threats."
"Find me at the bell frame once the stair is safe. If the clapper pin is missing, I can make old iron remember its job."

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: Tobias can repair the chapel bell if you bring him to the frame.

lines: "You cleared the stair? Then stop asking politely and show me the old frame."
"If it is only pin and wedge, I can make it answer. If it bites me, tell Susanna I was useful once."

effects.log: Tobias grabs his tool roll and runs for the wall stair.

onComplete.log: Tobias reaches the bell stair and disappears upward.

lines: "Bells are lazy things. They only have one job, and they still make men pull ropes for them."
"When every tower goes quiet at once, do not blame laziness. Blame hands, blades, or something worse than either."
"If I knew, I would be more useful and less frightened. Susanna says those two states rarely meet in the same man."
"The cult wanted the road quiet. I know that much. The part that scares me is whether they found it quiet when they arrived."
"Because Susanna has a voice that can make a grown man feel like an unpaid child."
"Also because Susanna was right, which you will not repeat unless you enjoy feeding her."
"Cruel work, cult-breaker. I knew I should have charged for that confession."
"I was a runner; scouts get better boots and worse orders."
"I carried notices, ration marks, warning scraps. Half the job was arriving before the lie did."

choices.label: Ask what he would do next

lines: "Hallowfen checkpoint is the next bad mile."
"If the wall still has honest ears, make them listen before the Choir does. Good luck, cult-breaker."
"If Susanna lets me, I watch the road by daylight and mark what is missing."
"If she does not, I will watch it anyway from somewhere she cannot see. I remain a fool. I am only becoming a supervised one."
